slides.title: Play Doom with friends in your browser
slides.body: Deploy your own copy to Cloudflare, open the Worker URL, and share room links with friends.

slides.title: Before you start
slides.body: Have these ready before opening Cloudflare's deploy flow.

slides.title: Create an empty GitHub repository
slides.body: Use your own GitHub account. Keep the repository empty so Cloudflare can copy the PlayDoom template into it.

slides.title: Click Deploy to Cloudflare
slides.body: Open playdoom.ossy.dev and start the deploy flow from the main button.

slides.title: Connect your own GitHub account
slides.body: Cloudflare will ask you to connect a Git provider. Use your own GitHub account, then give Cloudflare access to the repo you created in the previous step.
bullets: Select only the repo you created
Continue back to Cloudflare after GitHub authorizes access

slides.title: Review the application setup
slides.body: Use the empty repository as the destination. Review the generated Cloudflare resources and DOOM_KEY, then keep the defaults unless you want to rename the Worker.
bullets: Worker name: keep it or rename it
Keep the generated DOOM_KEY and resource settings

slides.title: Deploy and open your Worker URL
slides.body: When deployment finishes, Cloudflare shows your workers.dev URL.

slides.title: Share the link with friends
slides.body: Friends open the room link in their browser. Players should join before the host starts the match.

slides.title: Your copy. Your Cloudflare account.
slides.body: PlayDoom does not host your game. You own the deployed Worker and share the link when you want to play.
